seo.title: AI Agent Hallucination Detection in Amazon Bedrock Agents Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent hallucination detection in Amazon Bedrock Agents agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: AI Agent Hallucination in Amazon Bedrock Agents occurs when the agent generates factually incorrect or fabricated responses due to issues in action group Lambda responses, incomplete knowledge base retrievals, or guardrail bypasses. These hallucinations erode trust in enterprise deployments, particularly in multi-turn conversations where session state inconsistencies amplify errors. Vex provides robust post-processing detection by wrapping Bedrock's invoke_agent() API calls to catch and verify outputs against ground truth sources.

howItManifests.scenario: Action group Lambda functions returning malformed responses
howItManifests.symptom: Agent outputs contain JSON parsing errors or unexpected fields from Lambda action group responses
howItManifests.impact: Agent fails to execute subsequent actions or generates nonsensical responses based on invalid data

howItManifests.scenario: Knowledge base retrieval missing relevant documents due to chunking
howItManifests.symptom: Agent confidently cites non-existent document chunks or fabricates details not present in retrieved context
howItManifests.impact: Critical business decisions based on hallucinated document references and missing key information

howItManifests.scenario: Agent guardrails not catching domain-specific policy violations
howItManifests.symptom: Agent provides responses violating industry-specific compliance (HIPAA, FINRA) despite guardrails
howItManifests.impact: Regulatory non-compliance exposure and potential legal liabilities

howItManifests.scenario: Session state loss between multi-turn agent invocations
howItManifests.symptom: Agent forgets conversation context mid-session and generates contradictory or irrelevant responses
howItManifests.impact: Poor user experience and inability to complete complex multi-step workflows

detection.name: Lambda Response Schema Validation
detection.description: Detect malformed JSON from action group Lambda functions by validating against expected OpenAPI schema

detection.name: Knowledge Base Context Verification
detection.description: Cross-check agent citations against actual retrieved knowledge base chunks

detection.name: Session State Consistency Check
detection.description: Verify agent maintains conversation context across turns using session attributes

fixes.name: Structured Lambda Response Wrapper
fixes.description: Standardize action group Lambda outputs with strict JSON schema validation

fixes.name: Enhanced Knowledge Base Chunking
fixes.description: Implement semantic chunking and retrieval verification for Bedrock Knowledge Bases

fixes.name: Persistent Session State Management
fixes.description: Maintain conversation context using Bedrock sessionState with custom attributes

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Hallucination Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex automates hallucination detection by wrapping Bedrock Agent's invoke_agent() calls with comprehensive post-processing verification. It simultaneously validates Lambda responses against OpenAPI schemas, cross-references knowledge base retrievals with agent citations, monitors session state consistency, and enforces domain-specific guardrails. The platform provides real-time alerting, automated remediation workflows, and comprehensive dashboards for enterprise observability.

Integration requires only pip install boto3 vex-sdk and wrapping your existing invoke_agent() calls with vex.verify(). Vex handles the complexity of multi-modal verification (JSON schema, semantic similarity, compliance rules) while maintaining sub-second latency suitable for production workloads. Failed detections trigger fallback behaviors like knowledge base re-retrieval or human-in-the-loop escalation.
vexAutomation.code:
import boto3
from vex_sdk import VexVerifier

# Initialize clients
bedrock_agent = boto3.client('bedrock-agent-runtime')
vex = VexVerifier(api_key='your-vex-api-key')

@vex.verify(
    checks=[
        'bedrock_lambda_schema',
        'knowledge_base_citation',
        'session_context_loss',
        'compliance_guardrails'
    ],
    fallback='retry_with_hybrid_search',
    alert_on=['hallucination_detected']
)
def safe_agent_invoke(agent_id, alias_id, session_id, input_text, session_history):
    '''Vex-wrapped Bedrock Agent invocation'''
    response = bedrock_agent.invoke_agent(
        agentId=agent_id,
        agentAliasId=alias_id,
        sessionId=session_id,
        inputText=input_text,
        sessionState=build_session_state(session_history)
    )
    
    # Vex automatically validates:
    # 1. Lambda action group JSON schemas
    # 2. KB retrieval relevance & citation accuracy
    # 3. Session state consistency
    # 4. Domain compliance (HIPAA/FINRA/etc)
    
    return response

# Usage - Vex handles everything automatically
result = safe_agent_invoke(
    'YOUR_AGENT_ID',
    'YOUR_ALIAS_ID',
    'session-123',
    'Process insurance claim #ABC123'
    session_history
)
print(result['completion']['text'])

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Get started with `pip install vex-sdk` and wrap your Bedrock Agents in 5 minutes to eliminate hallucinations.
